articles.title: How Star Atlas Governance Took Shape
articles.audience: Readers seeking an accessible institutional history of Star Atlas governance
articles.summary: How the DAO, Foundation, Council and POLIS electorate developed as distinct parts of one governance system.
articles.description: A clear institutional history of Star Atlas governance from operational rollout through formal ratification and later amendments.

articles.title: Beyond the Vote: Success, Failure and Withdrawal in Star Atlas Governance
articles.audience: Readers researching Star Atlas proposal outcomes and governance history
articles.summary: Why election, failure, supersession, termination, cancellation and withdrawal require different historical labels.
articles.description: A mechanism-aware history of Star Atlas proposal outcomes that keeps passage separate from implementation.

articles.title: Following the Treasury: Authority, Funding and Payment Evidence
articles.audience: Readers following Star Atlas DAO funding and treasury history
articles.summary: A guide to treasury entities and the evidence needed to distinguish requests, authorization, payment and outcomes.
articles.description: An evidence-qualified history of Star Atlas treasury architecture, Ecosystem Fund awards and direct DAO authorizations.

articles.title: ATLAS and POLIS: Two Tokens, Different Institutions
articles.audience: Readers seeking a historical introduction to the Star Atlas token system
articles.summary: The distinct histories of ATLAS utility and POLIS governance across changing products, programs and measurements.
articles.description: A dated guide to Star Atlas token roles, locking, governance voting power and the limits of supply and emissions evidence.

articles.title: From SCORE to Starbased: The Evolution of Star Atlas Strategy
articles.audience: Players and researchers following the history of Star Atlas strategy products
articles.summary: How distinct products, programs and test surfaces formed the evolving Star Atlas strategy family.
articles.description: A lifecycle-qualified history of SCORE, Faction Fleet, SAGE Labs, Starbased, SAGE 3D and C4.

articles.title: The Showroom and Holosim: Two Windows Into Star Atlas
articles.audience: Readers comparing public Star Atlas client, simulation and test experiences
articles.summary: A parallel history of two public experiences with different access, build and release meanings.
articles.description: How the pre-alpha UE5 Showroom and browser-based Holosim opened different windows into the Star Atlas project.

articles.title: How Star Atlas Communicated Its Development
articles.audience: Readers and researchers navigating official Star Atlas communications
articles.summary: How official publication surfaces preserved different kinds of statements and why repetition is not independent verification.
articles.description: A source-critical history of official Star Atlas communication across long-form, social, support and governance surfaces.

articles.title: The Recorded Community: Atlas Brew, Town Halls and Star Atlas TV
articles.audience: Readers exploring the recorded institutional and community memory of Star Atlas
articles.summary: How recordings and timestamps preserve institutional memory even when speaker identity remains unknown.
articles.description: A history of Atlas Brew, Town Halls and replay archives with practical evidence rules for unattributed transcripts.

articles.title: The Institutions Behind Star Atlas
articles.audience: Readers seeking a plain-language map of the organizations behind Star Atlas
articles.summary: A plain-language map of the organizations that develop, govern and administer Star Atlas.
articles.description: How ATMTA, the DAO, Foundation and Council occupy different roles around one ecosystem.

articles.title: Welcome to the Galia Expanse
articles.audience: New and returning readers exploring the Star Atlas universe
articles.summary: An introduction to the factions, peoples, places and postwar political order of the Galia Expanse.
articles.description: A reader-first guide to Star Atlas lore grounded in the captured owned-affiliated canonical taxonomy.

articles.title: The People of Star Atlas: Players, Guilds, Creators and Community Memory
articles.audience: Readers interested in the human community and culture around Star Atlas
articles.summary: How players became organizers, publishers, competitors, critics and institution builders around an unfinished universe.
articles.description: A community history of Star Atlas players, guilds, creators and journalists with explicit identity and relevance boundaries.
